lovable_text: We have a clearly defined vision and mission that guides our decisions
note: Vision/mission are core inputs to the strategic planning process.

lovable_text: Our core values are well defined and lived throughout the organization
We have a specific culture that defines our organization's identity
note: Same theme as sp_2; both inherit from #46.

lovable_text: Our business model is well defined and market validated
note: Business model definition + validation maps to revenue model clarity.

lovable_text: Our business plans are aligned with the strategic plan
We have clearly defined short, mid, and long-term milestones
note: Milestones across horizons map best to cascading goals.

lovable_text: We have growth expectations and plans to achieve expected growth
note: Growth planning + capacity planning maps to FP&A.

lovable_text: Our financial statements are in place and available for decision making
We have a proper budgeting process on a yearly basis
Business performance is monitored based on the current budget
We have financial KPIs and ratios that are reviewed regularly
note: Could also map to #80; closer to financial reporting cadence.

lovable_text: We have defined, managed, and monitored our unit economics
We have a Financial Controller position with proper internal controls
note: Controller role is component of financial controls.

lovable_text: We have a proper internal audit role
note: Internal audit is part of financial reporting controls.

lovable_text: We manage and control working capital effectively
note: Working capital is core cash flow discipline.

lovable_text: We have a formal sourcing strategy for vendor management
We have a well-defined organizational structure
Leadership and ownership are well defined for each department
We have a consistent meeting rhythm
All teams clearly identify, discuss, and solve key issues
note: Issue resolution is part of decision-making discipline.

lovable_text: How does the organization collaborate with external partners to create value beyond its internal capabilities?
note: NEW — no clear bds-OS counterpart; metadata authored fresh: pnl=3, speed=2, dep=2, risk=false. Partnerships are variable in impact; slow to build.

lovable_text: We have clearly defined KPIs for all functional departments
We have formal processes to follow up on KPIs
We have proper mechanisms to review performance
We have a reliable BI platform for performance analysis
We have strategic KPIs available for top management
Our decision making process is based on objective data
We have consistent, repeatable and documented processes
Our systems and processes are documented and followed by all
We have well-defined labor structure per department
We have productivity metrics to ensure balanced labor templates
We have internal audit for operational effectiveness
Have work processes been redesigned from scratch to optimize collaboration between humans and technology (AI)?
note: NEW — no bds-OS counterpart; metadata authored fresh: pnl=4, speed=3, dep=4, risk=false. Significant productivity unlock; speed moderate; dependent on AI knowledge.

lovable_text: We have a clear performance evaluation process by competencies
We use a 9-blocker chart to evaluate performance and potential
Key employees are identified with succession plans in place
We have training plans according to employees' needs
We have written job descriptions for all positions
note: JDs are part of org design.

lovable_text: We have an induction program for new recruits
Training plans are well-defined for new recruits
We track 3-month retention rate for new hires
We track turnover rate and analyze causes
We conduct regular work environment surveys
We have plans and benefits to improve retention
We have salary compensation tabulators
We use market benchmarks for salary decisions
We ensure internal equity in compensation
Are reward systems designed to incentivize behaviors and practices that increase strategic value?
note: Aligned incentives is a comp design topic; could split into a new practice later.

lovable_text: How does the organization optimize the geographic and operational deployment of its talent and resources?
note: NEW — no bds-OS counterpart; metadata authored fresh: pnl=3, speed=2, dep=3, risk=false. Geographic talent strategy can save 20-40% on talent costs but slow to implement.

lovable_text: We have an infrastructure investment plan aligned with business objectives
We have defined service levels for platforms and applications
We have a software investment plan for optimal utilization
Our software is compatible and integrated with other systems
We have standardized software applications across the organization
note: Standardization is a vendor mgmt outcome.

lovable_text: We have a clear digitization roadmap
note: Digitization roadmap is a strategic infra topic.

lovable_text: Digitization is linked to our commercial strategies
We conduct cybersecurity risk assessments
We have a dedicated team and actions for cybersecurity
We examine data sets and analyze data for decision making
We have basic knowledge of AI types for identifying opportunities
note: NEW — AI literacy; metadata authored fresh: pnl=4, speed=3, dep=5, risk=true at level=2. Foundational; without it no AI opportunities are spotted; in 2026 leadership AI literacy is a risk floor.

lovable_text: Employees have access to AI tools and talent
note: NEW — AI tooling; metadata authored fresh: pnl=3, speed=4, dep=3, risk=false. Tools deploy quickly once literacy exists.

lovable_text: We have clear criteria for qualifying AI opportunities
note: NEW — AI investment criteria; metadata authored fresh: pnl=3, speed=3, dep=3, risk=false. Saves money by killing bad ideas early.

lovable_text: We have a roadmap for data analytics capabilities
To what extent does the organization use AI systems capable of planning and executing multiple steps autonomously?
note: NEW — Agentic AI; metadata authored fresh: pnl=4, speed=2, dep=4, risk=false. High-impact frontier; requires foundation.

lovable_text: We have a formal process for gathering and analyzing market data
We monitor and analyze competitors' activities
We identify new channels, products, and business opportunities
We utilize BI platforms for revenue performance analysis
We conduct systematic sales analysis
We track product availability metrics
note: Fill rate / back-order tracking is product quality.

lovable_text: We have a system for receiving regular customer feedback
We have a clear sales budget defined at multiple time intervals
We have a formal marketing and promotional plan
We conduct comprehensive salesforce performance analysis
We have variable compensation tied to sales performance
